hero.question: Will Tom Steyer win the California Governor Election in 2026?
hero.prob_take:
The market prices Steyer at 7.2%, essentially saying he has no shot in a primary crowded with higher-profile Democrats and no incumbent to run against. That's probably right for a guy whose $100M self-funding couldn't crack 1% in 2020, but California's open seat in 2026 changes the calculus enough that this is underpriced by 2-3 points.

Watch whether Newsom consolidates early money and endorsements by mid-2025, which would confirm the market's thesis that Steyer becomes background noise rather than a credible spoiler.

daily_take.headline: Steyer's California Comeback Bet Just Got Real
daily_take.deck: Tom Steyer's 2026 California governor odds jumped 4.2 points to 7.2% on $856K volume, signaling fresh money believes he's viable again after his 2018 primary flop. The market's wrong. Steyer burned through $100M of his own cash last time and finished fourth. Nothing structurally changed except maybe the field looks weaker. The smart play is selling this pop.
